Arc Flash Study- a grandeur or mandate?

Blog › blog

The latest update about Arc Flash studies in the USA describes how facilities fall behind in reassuring the electrical safety of their workers. Check this:

 

The Partnership for Electrical Safety invigorates a guidance report from OSHA on comfortable arc-rated clothing in agreement with NFPA 70E standards.

 

In the US alone, more than 750,000 electricians do not have proper PPE or safety equipment. The way things are now, the PES is brewing many initiatives to ensure that workers in this category enjoy safe and risk-free working conditions. This piece of news is a great relief for these workers as Congress has asked the OSHO to develop a new Arc Flash Guidance report. Once issued, the PES will support the employers to comply with the OSHO standards. In the US alone, more than 750,000 electricians do not have proper PPE or safety equipment. The way things are now, the PES is brewing many initiatives to ensure that workers in this category enjoy safe and risk-free working conditions. This piece of news is a great relief for these workers as Congress has asked the OSHO to develop a new Arc Flash Guidance report. Once issued, the PES will support the employers to comply with the OSHO standards.

The PES is an organization in the USA focusing on educating employees on safety standards working near electrical equipment. The organization works to empower individuals working near electrical equipment by encouraging them to use standard PPEs and arc-rated clothing. In this framework, the PES works in collaboration with the OSHA, Congress, and the Department of Labor to drive more safety for these workers, and emphasize the worker’s perspective as well as that of the employers in adopting safer working conditions. However, there are still many gaps to be filled and companies and employers must understand that Electrical safety and an Arc Flash Study are neither a grandeur nor a mandate, but a moral responsibility.

 

Some of the arguments why facilities think they don’t need an Arc Flash Study and Analysis

 


Facility owners often argue about why they needn’t conduct an Arc Flash Study and Analysis every five years. The whys and wherefores include the fact that their electricians are certified, they know their work, no major mishaps have happened in their facility so far, and so on. This misconception that something needs to take place to clear the safety requirements drives many accidents. Lack of Awareness of Arc Flash Studies and compliant codes is what makes these facility owners think otherwise. Educating the workforce, employers, and other facility owners can bring about a major change in this perspective.

Every year the EFSI( Electrical Safety Foundation International) celebrates May month as the electrical safety month of the year. This initiative is undertaken to inform and educate people in general about electrical safety inside and outside of any facility. Arc Flash awareness is an integral part of this initiative.

The widespread adoption of electrical energy to power office spaces, residential buildings, and even cars has necessitated the use of safe practices and awareness of potential dangers. Regular maintenance and analysis can minimize the dangers. Being a sustainable source of power, we need to address these issues in hand at the right time.

“ If it weren’t for Electricity, we would all be watching Television by candlelight”, this quote by the famous humorist, George Gobel, makes us all recall how pivotal this discovery is for humankind.

Our electricity may be green or gray, although we often rely heavily on this form of energy. On the flip side, there are disguised threats looming around Electricity. One potential risk of using Electricity is Arc Flashes, which must be addressed promptly. We must also understand that electrical equipment and wires also age and wear down like people do. Unexpected intruders, unidentified faults, or even human intervention in an electrical system can have a huge blowback to the system and can result in hazards. To Make allowances for a smooth and hazard-free workplace, top companies conduct Arc Flash Studies to rate their electrical system. Residential setups are also not exempted from the dangers of electrical system hazards and need Arc Flash studies time and again.

 

What exactly is an Arc Flash?

 

Arc Flashes are the most underrated kind of electrical hazard that can have many ill effects. They happen when high electric discharges take place between the loose ends of a conductor, as the air in between offers the least resistance to the flow of current. This is accompanied by a huge amount of heat, often described as much above the temperature on the surface of the sun, to cause an Arc and subsequent blasting. The most common practice to avoid these is to conduct an Arc Flash Study to identify the faults and rectify them.

Let us understand the danger lurking around Arc Flashes in detail through a simulated industry case study. Consider a company that hires an electrician to check the fault in the system. He is equipped with the tools and expertise to rectify it. For some reason, one of the tools slipped from his hand and landed on the circuit. This may create a short circuit in the system. An Arc flash incident is thus created and most likely, a fire breaks out. However, the electrician is not wearing his prescribed safety equipment. What happens in such cases is disturbing to think in the first place. There is a fairly good chance that the person loses his life. Alternatively, if the company had already done an Arc Flash Study and Analysis, it could prescribe safety wear for the electrician, thus curbing the danger of losing a life.


One case study and we are clear on the fact that Arc Flash Studies are mandatory. Different countries follow different compliance guidelines that are relevant for Arc Flash Study and Analysis.

 

Standards for Arc Flash Study and Analysis

 

Various codes and standards are defined for Arc Flash Study, and comprehending this is crucial for performing the same. Mandatory compliances like OSHO, NFPA 70E, IEEE 1584, etc are the standard codes for Arc Flash Study Analysis. In addition, some of the situations where it is mandatory to do an Arc Flash study are:

  • When company insurance necessitates Arc Flash Study reports
  • When major alterations are done in the electrical power system.
  • If a major hazard or damage has already happened to the electrical system.

The company must be responsible for safeguarding the safety of the manpower as they rely on the company to offer a hazard-free working atmosphere. It is thus, the moral duty of each company or facility in general to leverage an Arc Flash Study and Analysis consulting Service.

 

Who can perform the Arc Flash Study?

 

The zillion-dollar question- who can do an Arc Flash Study and Analysis? Only experienced professionals with an in-depth understanding of compliance codes like the OSHO, NFPA 70E, and IEEE 1584 are qualified to perform Arc Flash studies and Analysis.


Care Labs offers cost-effective and code-compliant electrical safety and engineering solutions to companies across the USA, Europe, Canada, and Pan-Asia. ETAP 22.5 software is used to do Arc Flash study and evaluation.
Care Labs’ approach to an Arc Flash Study follows these steps:

  • Evaluate the components, operational procedures, and settings of the electrical system.
  • Utilize the ETAP 22.5 application to get information about the Arc Flash boundaries, and incident energy levels, and recommend PPEs for workers.
  • All the findings and recommendations are included in a report customized to a specific location’s standards.
  • Expert professionals from Care Labs offer counsel and suggestions on implementing necessary system adjustments to align with the standards.

Care Labs is a pioneer in offering comprehensive Arc Flash Study and Analysis. With the intent of helping facilities and big MNCs achieve electrical operational excellence, Care Labs employs the latest technology to detect faults. With a home office in Dubai, Care Labs provides electrical safety services in many locations across the globe.
Care Labs’ Arc Flash Study and Analysis aligns with standards like OSHA and NFPA 70 E/ 70B. The company uses the latest ETAP 22.5 software for Arc Flash study and Analysis.
As of now, Care Labs has provided services to clients from District Cooling plants, Utility Generation, Sewage Treatment Plants, Supermarkets, malls and commercial buildings, and so on. With ongoing multifarious projects from various industries, the company is one of the leading choices for Power system Analysis, testing, calibration, and Inspection.

Final word

Companies around the world must comprehend the significance of conducting an Arc Flash Study and Analysis every five years. This is not just to clear the decks, but to safeguard the workers from the potential danger of Arc Flashes. It is extremely dangerous for the manpower if an Arc flash incident happens in a facility.
Although many awareness programs and mandates are being introduced regularly, any facility owner must consider this as his/her moral responsibility.
Companies like Care Labs are adept in offering services in this area. With the latest software applications and high-end tools, Care Labs has made many companies a safe place to work. As the saying goes, Better Safe than Sorry, take the first step to vaccinate your workplace from Electrical hazards.

Energy Conservation by Auditing Power Quality

Blog › casestudy

OVERVIEW:

Prior to the execution of this solution, a well-known fertilizer company experienced a number of unanticipated outages caused by disruptions in the supplying utility. The power management control system at the facility had degraded and no longer provided appropriate visibility and control of the interconnection tie to the Grid.

REQUIREMENT:

Care Labs was contracted to undertake power system studies in order to assess the unplanned outages, operational inefficiencies, and make recommendations to improve system performance.

The scope also includes the replacement of Bay Control Units (BCU) and Human–Machine Interface (HMI) / Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) systems. Care Labs also assisted the firm in resolving the frequent failure of previously implemented EMS/SCADA, automating their operations, and reducing load shedding.

CHALLENGES:

  1. Lack of adequate electrical data and power system model at the firm.
  2. The history of events was not documented.
  3. Electrical data in Distributed Control System (DCS) was missing.
  4. Process Interruptions and frequent unplanned outages due to improper protective device settings.
  5. Nuisance Load shedding triggers induced by power grid frequency variations.
  6. Excessive termination of processes during load shedding events.
  7. Outdated and Faulty SCADA system
  8. Inadequate frequency relay setting and operation, as well as improper generator operating mode.
  9. High voltage drop as a result of non-sequential motor starting
  10. There is no infrastructure in place to carry out root cause analysis and effectively monitor energy usage
  11. Failures in substation control and HMI
  12. The operator has insufficient system expertise.

SOLUTION

Care Labs and the fertilizer firm collaborated to equip the facility with an integrated power management solution based on a novel model-based approach:

  1. Creating and evaluating an electrical model for the whole facility
  2. Conducting extensive power system analysis
  3. Identifying operational deficiencies
  4. Replacing old BCUs with new ones
  5. Segregating and interfacing all electrical data
  6. PMS tuning and implementation of the same model
  7. Setting up a user-friendly HMI for easy system operation
  8. Installation of various interlocks to prevent operational and human errors.
  9. Advanced monitoring and predictive simulation
  10. Improving grid synchronization to avoid unnecessary system shutdowns
  11. Estimation of state and load
  12. Event reenactment and root cause analysis
  13. Intelligent load shedding and validation
  14. Synchronization of the grid
  15. Web-based HMI dashboards installation

RESULTS

The power quality audit has been beneficial to the firm in terms of operations and validation of their plant’s power system.

  1. With predictive analysis, overall productivity has been increased and unnecessary system shutdowns have been drastically reduced.
  2. Reduced process downtime from one hour to 15 minutes
  3. Increased the efficiency of the operation by 75%
  4. ROI was 105.6% after the second year.

Over $41,000 in monthly savings on synchronization operations.

Improving Reliability Through Electrical Safety Audit at a Retail Company

Blog › casestudy

OVERVIEW:

Compliance can be difficult for retailers, especially for the ones who have large property portfolios scattered across diverse locations and with a wide range of statutory obligations. Electrical Safety Inspection & Audit of Electrical Installation, in particular, necessitates specialized knowledge and a rigorous approach to ensure compliance.

REQUIREMENT:

Our client, a prominent retail organization with over 50 branches in the UAE, was looking for a reliable compliance partner. This portfolio encompasses 23 locations, where an adequate electrical safety inspection, including risk assessments and audit of electrical installation, would be crucial to ensure the safety of employees and consumers. They approached Care Labs, experts in testing, inspection, and certification with a dedicated team of experts operating globally to assist them.

SOLUTION:

Care Labs offered our client a comprehensive compliance package that includes the physical inspection, thermography testing, earth test, and power quality analysis of panels. The client’s services are delivered by a team of skilled technicians that collaborate closely with the client’s on-site team to guarantee effective and proactive delivery.

Care Labs has been delivering comprehensive Electrical Safety Inspection services for the entire portfolio of locations as part of this solution for over five years, incorporating:

  • Rigorous evaluations and assessments to identify potential electrical hazards.
  • Inspecting the working environment, work processes, and facilities.
  • Reviewing the specification, operation, and maintenance records of the protection system.
  • Inspecting the installation for compliance with applicable rules, regulations, and standards.
  • Maintaining a documented record of the plan of action.
  • Proposing appropriate risk mitigation methods

Care Labs has executed over 20 projects for the client, assisting with programs around UAE, ranging from basic safety tests to major installation component replacement.

RESULTS:

As part of this solution, Care Labs conducted a thorough risk assessment, after which the re-inspection and survey schedule were re-engineered to ensure that resources were targeted on the highest risk areas. Lower risk locations stayed compliant while being serviced at a lower cost.

This allowed us to show genuine cost savings as well as a demonstrable improvement in service at high-risk areas. We were also able to decrease re-inspection expenses by integrating these inspections with forthcoming Planned Preventive Maintenance (PPM) inspections, obviating the need for a second visit.

Other benefits include:

  • Complete assurance in all aspects of safety and reliability.
  • Reduce the risk of expensive litigation should a product cause injury
  • Reliable and trustworthy report about your electrical system using industry-recognized ETAP software
  • Periodic risk assessments throughout the portfolio to identify the optimal use of resources in order to conserve energy.
  • Cost savings are realized through the use of combined services and a risk-based approach for assessment.

Prioritizing a Safe Work Environment at a Multinational Technology Company

Blog › casestudy

Overview

An UAE-based multinational technology company that focuses on information technology, cloud computing, digital streaming, and artificial intelligence needed help in the maintenance of its worker safety commitment. With numerous distribution facilities around the world, this company partnered with Care Labs to make sure operations at their 615,000 square-foot distribution facility in UAE were being performed safely and perfectly. Care Labs helped in meeting the objectives of the company by delivering low voltage preventative maintenance (LVPM) and planning and fixing an Arc Flash relay system, ensuring efficient, safe, and sound operations for the company’s distribution facility.

Requirement

The technology agglomerate has always been committed to the safety of its employees. During opening discussions with Care Labs, they preferred to create a safe working environment for their 1,000+ employees at their new distribution facility. They required help in developing long-lasting, dependable operations and were focusing on preventative maintenance to mitigate safety risks and sudden downtime in which each hour lost sums up to $100k.

Solution

Care Labs executed the following services on 4,000A during a 12-hour shutdown window:

  • LVPM, which included:
  • Cleaning, torquing, and tightening bus and lug connections
  • Secondary injection test on circuit breakers
  • Inspecting all devices and connections
  • Installing Infrared windows with 10 Fluke windows per lineup.
  • Designing, manufacturing, installing, and testing Arc Flash relay system.
  • Updating Arc Flash data for the main switchboard near to relay installation.
  • Reporting the results and making additional recommendations.

While Care Labs were on spot performing LVPM and an Arc Flash inspection, there were problems with re-closing the 4,000A pressure post-testing, which resulted in replacing and testing the new 4,000A switch. Care Labs were able to provide the group of experts required to make the explanatory repairs, bring back power, and recommence operations safely at the facility.

Results

Care Labs carry on with providing LVPM and Arc Flash inspections. Therefore, the company has seen 100- 200cal rated sections of a switchboard lowered to 3-8cal so that facility managers can operate devices in the main switchboard to carry out preventative maintenance work in their windows. Earlier, they had to perform a facility shut down for the entire space to carry out their work. With Care Labs’s Arc Flash proficiency and local knowledge, the company was able to create a solution in tandem offering a strong tool for long-lasting, reliable operations which can provide incredible results.

Resolving Safety Issues with Arc Flash and Lockout/Tagout Services at a Leading Medical Company

Blog › casestudy

OVERVIEW

A UAE-based leading medical company, has a 320-acre site accommodating around 700 employees, who work across the biopharmaceutical business or in one of the business support functions (such as finance or HR).

The site also acts as a hub for some of the company’s commercial operations.

REQUIREMENT

Care Labs received a query from the company’s facility management to carry out an arc flash risk assessment at their UAE business headquarters. The arc flash study was to be carried out in compliance with UAE Legislation using the benchmark IEEE 1584 and NFPA 70E and to assist them in meeting with the buildings insurers requirements.

SOLUTION

Care Labs carried out a free-of-cost, on-scene pre-evaluation to understand the extent of the scope of works at the company’s commercial headquarters to assess the size of their network. We provided a fixed price quotation for an Arc Flash Risk Assessment Study with project milestones and a completion deadline agreed with the client, with an interim early draft provided in two months to assist the facilities management team.

Electronic copies of the electrical network one-line drawings were provided to the company to enlarge the electrical network on our tablet making use of the ETAP software preceding the site data collection visit.

We carried out the discovery phase of the work, collecting and verifying data on the electrical distribution system by comprehensively surveying the site’s electrical systems and differentiating and adjusting the electrical distribution system model wherever necessary on our tablet.

The details collected and verified during the site data collection process with our tablet andETAPsoftware were downloaded into our ETAP modeling software.

RESULT

After the initial site data collection and verification visit, an early provisional draft of the arc flash study was provided in line with the arc flash study requirements.

Following verification and provision of outstanding data a full arc flash analysis was carried out in the company including:

  • Short circuit analysis
  • Protective device coordination
  • Arc flash incident energy
  • Arc flash boundary distance

Any situations where the 3-phase prospective short circuit current exceeded the tolerable capacity of protective devices were focused in the report. Protection device time v/s current characteristic curves showing any instances of miscoordination were provided. Incident energy levels and arc flash boundary distances were listed for all components included in the scope of the study.

Recent one-line diagrams for the electrical network were provided to the facilities management team that included Site Data, Fault Study, and Arc Flash Study Analysis.

Incident energy hazard mitigation analysis was then performed and suitable options and order of magnitude costs were given for the reduction of the incident energy where it was above 8 Cals/cm2.

Care labs presented the Arc Flash Report via video conferencing with the company.

Arc Flash labels to the UAE standard were issued for application to the electrical component switchgear and enclosures.

The Importance of Relay Coordination in Power System

Blog › blog

Introduction

Relays are protective elements that help control faults in a power system. Proper relay coordination helps isolate the fault sections from healthier sections in a power system.

Being a fundamental part of ensuring reliability and operational continuity, the strategic configuration of protective relays is imperative in the design of a power system.

One way to ensure that the relays are working properly is to conduct a relay coordination study at different stages of the power system’s lifecycle.

The importance of relay coordination study in a power system can be summarized as:

  • Ensure System Reliability

If the relays in a power system don’t work in coordination, the chances of disruptions in the system escalate. On the other hand, proper coordination of these protective devices ensures that fault is isolated on time maintaining power supply in the healthier sections.

  • Protect system elements

Short circuits and overloads are common faults in any power system. They can damage critical components like transformers, cables, generators, etc. Protective device coordination ensures that these devices are isolated from faults and operational safety is maintained.

  • Prevent Cascading Faults

If the protective relays work in a coordinated manner, small faults can be prevented from escalating into large-scale incidents. Relay coordination thus protects the system from cascading faults.

  • Ensure Workplace Safety

Electrical hazards are a serious threat in any large facility and industry, as a whole. Arc flashes,  short circuits, etc can result in injuries to personnel and even fatalities. Relay coordination helps prevent this to a large extent by considerably reducing the faults in the system persist.

  • Minimize Downtime

Maintaining uptime is critical to industries like hospitals and data centers. What bet way to achieve this than isolating fault conditions? Vital operations can be sustained and uptime maintained if the relays in the power system work in cohesion.

“A relay Coordination study entails a systematic evaluation to ensure that the protective relays are working in a coordinated manner in the electrical system.”

  • Reduce Arc flash incidents

Arc flashes are serious threats to electrical safety in any facility. Relay coordination can help mitigate the arc flashes to a great extent by reducing the incident energy levels and limiting the fault durations effectively.

  • Optimize the system performance

Disruptions, that are uncalled for result in reducing the power quality in the system. Relay coordination is one of the key factors in restoring the overall efficiency of the power system.

  • Adapt to new upgrades in the system

Ensuring proper coordination of relays helps the power system grow and evolve. A well-coordinated relay system can accommodate changes in loads and updated settings and configurations.

  • Compliance with standards

A well-maintained electrical system follows the standards of electrical safety in the USA like OSHA, NFPA 70E, and IEEE. Adopting the best practices and frameworks put forward by these standards helps facilities ensure safety and reduce legal liabilities by and large.

  • Ensures seamless Renewable Energy integration

As industries are focusing more on sustainability and renewable energy, relay coordination ensures that fault currents contributed by inverters and distributed generation are managed effectively. It also supports the reliability of hybrid systems and microgrids.

“A relay coordination study in the USA is an essential part of the maintenance, operation, and design phases of a power system. The study involves evaluating fault current levels, determining appropriate relay settings (e.g., pickup currents, time delays), and analyzing time-current characteristic (TCC) curves to achieve selective tripping.”

Care Labs, USA

Relay coordination study must be adopted by industries across the USA to ensure that the system and workers are protected. Care Labs offers comprehensive power system study and analysis services in the USA. We use technology like ETAP for relay coordination simulations and help companies overcome the challenges.

Early Signs of Relay Coordination Changes in the System

Facilities must identify the early changes in relay coordination and take necessary steps to restore system stability.  Some of the signs that these protective devices are not working properly are:

  • Multiple relay trips for a single-fault event
  • High fault currents when the system is modified
  • Changes in system configuration need to be analyzed properly
  • Increased fault clearance times result in strain on the system components.
  • Changes in load patterns
  • Outdated settings not complying with the latest standards.
  • Equipment aging leads to performance issues
  • Fluctuating voltages affecting the system stability

If your facility is exhibiting any of the above signs, feel free to contact us. Our professionals will guide you in transforming the power system challenges into incredible solutions.

As industries are focusing more on sustainability and renewable energy, relay coordination ensures that fault currents contributed by inverters and distributed generation are managed effectively. It also supports the reliability of hybrid systems and microgrids.

“A relay coordination study in the USA is an essential part of the maintenance, operation, and design phases of a power system. The study involves evaluating fault current levels, determining appropriate relay settings (e.g., pickup currents, time delays), and analyzing time-current characteristic (TCC) curves to achieve selective tripping.”

Care Labs, USA

Relay coordination study must be adopted by industries across the USA to ensure that the system and workers are protected. Care Labs offers comprehensive power system study and analysis services in the USA. We use technology like ETAP for relay coordination simulations and help companies overcome the challenges.

Early Signs of Relay Coordination Changes in the System

Facilities must identify the early changes in relay coordination and take necessary steps to restore system stability.  Some of the signs that these protective devices are not working properly are:

Multiple relay trips for a single-fault event
High fault currents when the system is modified
Changes in system configuration need to be analyzed properly
Increased fault clearance times result in strain on the system components.
Changes in load patterns
Outdated settings not complying with the latest standards.
Equipment aging leads to performance issues
Fluctuating voltages affecting the system stability

If your facility is exhibiting any of the above signs, feel free to contact us. Our professionals will guide you in transforming the power system challenges into incredible solutions.

Three Mile Island Blast (1979)

Blog › casestudy

The Three Mile Island Unit 2 reactor, near Middletown, Pa., partially melted down on March 28, 1979. This was the most serious accident in U.S. commercial nuclear power plant operating history.

What Happened?

  • The plant experienced a failure in the secondary, non-nuclear section of the plant (one of two reactors on the site).
  • Either a mechanical or electrical failure prevented the main feedwater pumps from sending water to the steam generators that remove heat from the reactor core.
  • This caused the plant’s turbine generator and then the reactor itself to automatically shut down. Immediately, the pressure in the primary system began to increase.
  • To control that pressure, the pilot-operated relief valve opened. The valve should have closed when the pressure fell to proper levels, but it became stuck open.
  • Other instruments available to plant staff provided inadequate or misleading information. As a result, plant staff assumed that instruments showed water level was high enough and the core was properly covered with water too. That wasn’t the case.
  • Unaware of the stuck-open relief valve and unable to tell if the core was covered with cooling water, the staff took a series of actions that uncovered the core.

Analysing the Root Cause:

  1. Failure of automatic control valve
  2. Equipment and instrument malfunctions
  3. Human errors in operating procedures

Impact:

  1. The TMI-2 reactor was destroyed.
  2. Radiation-induced health effects in the area surrounding the plant.

What could have been done to avoid this?

  1. Adopting preventive maintenance to eliminate any potential machinery defects which could lead to failure.
  2. Deploy a condition monitoring system to gain insight into the health of key assets
  3. Carry out safety inspections periodically

What should be done to avoid such incidents in the future:

Have a third party professional electrical installation safety testing and inspection service done for your establishment. Include Electrical Safety Audit and EICR Certification to evaluate electrical wiring, connections, and systems for potential electrical hazards, design, and structural flaws. 

Provide comprehensive safety training to all operators and electricians.

Practise workplace’s safety culture and develop strategies to increase their safety levels

Every time an Electrical Contractor completes a new electrical installation, extension, or modification to an existing installation he/she is obliged to test and certify that the installation complies with current standards.

Upgrading Your Power System? Don’t Ignore the Short Circuit analysis.

Blog › blog

Introduction

A short circuit, especially in a large power system, is a serious concern. When expanding a power system, you must ensure that the existing system can withstand these faults and choose new upgrades based on a short circuit analysis of the system.

What exactly is a short circuit analysis?

Short circuit analysis is a study performed on a power system to measure how it withstands faults. The study reveals potential vulnerabilities in the system and determines the safe rating of the protective devices. This study is important as it helps ensure that the upgraded system operates safely, reliably, and complies with industry standards.

Care Labs offers specialized Short circuit analysis for industries in the USA. These services intend to help industries ensure the safety and peak performance of their power systems.

Why is a short circuit study important for upgrading a power system?

Upgrading a power system equates to adding more system components which might affect the existing protection schemes. Changes in fault current levels and load distribution can seriously affect the system’s performance and protection. In such a scenario, fault current analysis helps get insights into various aspects.

  • How fault current changes impact the upgrades: Short circuit analysis gives a numerical analysis of new fault currents and identifies the ratings of the upgrades.
  • What are the changes needed in the protective devices: New upgrades mean changes in protective relays and circuit breakers. The study gives a clear picture of these devices that help avoid disruptions.
  • The Thermal and mechanical stress on critical components in the system: Short circuit analysis reveals whether the newly added system and configurations can withstand the design limits during adult conditions.
  • Compliance with the standards: While any changes in the system need to abide bu the electrical safety codes, short circuit analysis in the USA is an essential part of maintaining this.

Why prioritize a short circuit analysis in the USA?

With a short circuit analysis, the above concerns can be effectively addressed along with the following benefits:

  • Ensure electrical safety
  • System reliability
  • Optimized performance
  • Reduced costly disruptions

What are the steps in short circuit analysis during system upgrades?

  • Data Collection- Develop a single-line diagram of the system and collect information regarding load changes and network configuration.
  • Fault Current Calculation- Calculate the new fault current at every node in the system, and analyze the various fault scenarios.
  • Equipment Evaluation- Compare the fault currents and evaluate the stress on various critical components like transformers, busbars, and cables. Ensure that the protective devices are working in coordination.
  • Report generation and recommendations- A detailed report of all the data collected, methods and tools used, and suggestions are documented and submitted. Following these recommendations help comply with the various local and international standards.

What are the risks of skipping short circuit analysis during system Upgrades?

Failing to conduct a short circuit analysis during upgrades can have serious consequences:

  1. Equipment Failures: Underrated circuit breakers may fail to interrupt fault currents, leading to catastrophic damage.
  2. Extended Outages: Improper coordination of protective devices can cause widespread system outages.
  3. Non-Compliance: Lack of adherence to updated codes and standards can result in fines or legal liabilities. Safety Hazards: Increased risk of arc flash incidents, exposing personnel to severe injuries or fatalities.

A short circuit analysis is a premium ticket to electrical safety and compliance in the USA. When you upgrade your power system, you must ensure that the new and old components align with the goals of safety, reliability, and efficiency. From utility industries to data centers, every facility must ensure that their systems and personnel are free from electrical hazards.

As a final note, expanding your power system should be taken as an opportunity to perfect it. A short circuit analysis is a proactive method to ensure this. So, don’t disregard this by keeping it as an afterthought. Instead, make this a key component of your safety protocol.

Sign up to learn more on how Care Lab’s short circuit analysis in the USA helps you upgrade better!

What are the Key Insights Obtained from Load Flow Analysis?

Blog › Electrical Safety

What is Load Flow Analysis

It is one of the most difficult yet critical analyses of the power system. It is a computational analysis to determine the flow of power through an electrical system. Power Flow Analysis determines how both active and reactive powers are distributed across the system, thereby evaluating its steady-state performance.

Care Labs is a power system consultant company. We offer a range of studies and analyses for power systems across the USA, one among them being the load flow analysis.

Importance of Load Flow Analysis in the USA

A load flow analysis ensures that the current and voltage in the system are well within the prescribed limits. The main outcomes of the power flow study are:

  • Identify the power losses, voltage drops, and other bottlenecks.
  • Design and optimize the existing or new power system
  • Maintain stability in the voltage levels and power distribution
  • Make informed decisions for future expansion through simulations and analyzing the situations.

Information Obtained from Load Flow Analysis

  • The magnitude of Voltage and phase angle at each bus

This information helps identify where the voltage levels are fluctuating. This is an important date that helps in regulating voltage and maintaining power quality and system stability.

  • The Active and Reactive Power through the Transmission Lines

Load Flow analysis gives us the direction of the flow of power in transmission lines. This information tells us where power optimization is needed to reduce the losses.

  • System Losses

Power losses in transmission line lines and transformers. This data helps in identifying the high-loss areas and helps in system upgrades.

  • Loading levels of transmission lines and transformers

Analyzing the loading levels of these helps in preventing equipment damage from overloading. This in turn improves the lifespan of the power system as a whole.

  • Power Factor at Different Buses

Load flow analysis offers data on the active and reactive power generated. This tells us how the generators are working and helps in balancing the load demand.

How can we use this information from Load Flow Analysis in the USA?

The main goal of load flow analysis is to determine the steady-state operating conditions of the system. With this information, we can

  • Optimize the system
  • Regulate the voltage
  • Contingency planning
  • Future expansion planning
  • Comply with the standards
  • Methods of Doing Load Flow Analysis

Power Flow studies can done through various methods:

  • Gauss-Seidel Method: Simple and easy to implement but slow for large systems.
  • Newton-Raphson Method: Highly accurate and faster for complex networks.
  • Fast Decoupled Method: Efficient for large-scale systems with high-speed computation needs.

Care Labs’ advantage in load flow analysis in the USA

Load Flow Analysis is a very important part of power system study and analysis. It offers insights into how your power system can be maintained, optimized, and expanded in the future. However, it is also important to choose the best power system consultants to offer the same.

Our team of professionals has the right credentials and experience that help companies convert their obstacles into premium solutions. We use ETAP as our load flow structure to generate impeccable results and reports.  We offer:

  • Comprehensive load flow analysis in the USA with all-inclusive reports with voltage profiles and recommendations
  • Tailored solutions suited for the industry to meet their specific requirements.
  • Adherence to compliance standards and codes for electrical safety in the USA.

Conclusion

By understanding how power flows through the network, engineers can optimize system design, prevent failures, and maintain reliable power delivery under various operating conditions. The information obtained from a load flow study is invaluable for engineers and system operators. It helps stakeholders make informed decisions related to the power system and prepare for future challenges.

Which of these Facts About Arc Flashes are True?

Blog › Electrical Safety

Introduction

In electric hazards, arc flashes are among the most dreadful threats. Despite their criticality, many myths surround arc flashes. Although IEEE and NFPA continuously strive to enhance awareness, five to ten arc flashes happen in the USA daily.

We, at Care Labs understand the importance of maintaining a healthy electrical infrastructure. To help companies accomplish their workplace safety goals, we offer comprehensive arc flash analysis for industries in the USA.

Let us first understand what arc flashes are and debunk the myths one after one.

What is an Arc flash?

An electric arc is formed when a current takes a nonprescribed path through the air between two conductors. When the electric arc persists, arc flashes happen, accompanied by huge amounts of heat and light.

The common characteristics of arc flashes are:

  • Temperatures beyond 35,000°F, which is three times that on the surface of the sun.
  • Blinding light that is 130 times brighter than sunlight.
  • Pressure wave close to that from a grenade explosion.

Fact 1: Arc flash is an explosive release of energy

Yes, Arc Flash is an explosive release of electrical energy. A fault such as an insulation breakdown or human error results in current flowing through the air gap between conductors. This rapid release of energy can cause injuries or even fatalities to workers close by. Equipment gets damaged and the entire operation can get disrupted. Hence, facilities must do arc flash analysis to identify the areas of hidden risks.

Fact 2: Arc Flash happens only in High Voltage Systems

This is a myth as arc flashes can occur in high-voltage as well as low-voltage systems. Arc flashes happening in low voltage systems as low as 120V also have the potential to cause injuries. Although the low-voltage arc flashes are most underrated, they can be as severe as high-voltage ones. Arc Flash hazard assessment reveals the severity of the potential arc flashes.

Fact 3: Arc Flash hazard can be completely prevented

This is again a myth as they cannot be completely prevented. We can identify the key areas of high risks, and adopt the best possible safety measures to prevent damage to personnel and equipment. Regular arc flash analysis as outlined by OSHA, NFPA, IEEE, etc can ensure workplace safety and compliance.

Fact 4: Arc Flash Labels are optional

No, they are not. Arc Flash labels are mandatory for any electrical equipment. Arc flash labels provide details like the incident energy level, arc flash boundary, PPE requirements, etc. They are the warning stickers for electrical workers that help them to repair and do the maintenance work.

Fact 5: Arc flash PPE can save lives

Yes, PPE or Personal Protective Equipment is lifesaving clothing used by workers to protect themselves from the dangers of arc flash hazards. It constitutes arc-rated clothing, safety goggles, hearing protection, voltage-rated gloves, and so on. Primarily, there are four categories of arc flash PPE based on the severity of the hazards.

Fact 6: Arc Flash Boundaries help workers protect them from getting injured

True. An arc flash boundary is the safe distance from an energized electrical system. A clear understanding of arc flash boundaries helps prevent burns and fatalities when working on an electrical system. As recommended by the NFPA 70E, there are three boundaries to consider. These are restricted approach, limited approach, and arc flash boundaries.

Fact 7: Regular maintenance reduces the risks of arc flashes

This statement is true. Preventive maintenance reduces the risks of arc flashes. A well-maintained electrical system is less prone to hazards. Regular arc flash analysis in the USA helps companies stay compliant and avoid costly hazards that can injure personnel and equipment.

Fact 8: Arc Flash Training is only for electricians

This is a misconception. Arc flash training creates awareness, which is imperative for all workers to behave and protect themselves in case of hazards. Facility managers, maintenance staff, and other workers must be educated on the signs of electrical flaws and hazards.

Fact 9: The Biggest Cause for Arc Flashes is Human Errors

True, human errors significantly contribute to arc flash hazards. Mishandling tools, wrong maintenance procedures, and other mistakes result in arc flashes. Lack of following the LOTO procedures, touching energized components, etc can also lead to arc flash hazards.

Fact 10: The biggest misconception- An arc flash study or analysis is only needed when there are any previous incidents.

Arc flash analysis is the most underrated electrical safety protocol. It is a safety ticket to ensure that your facility and its workers are secure. Regulatory bodies like OSHA and NFPA 70E issues update guidelines periodically to enhance workplace safety. They emphasize the need for companies to conduct arc flash study every five years.

The Care Labs’ role in Arc flash Analysis in the USA

Care Labs, based in Dubai offers power system study and Analysis services in the USA. We provide premium arc flash analysis that aligns with the company’s goals and safety standards. With the most advanced software like ETAP, our arc flash analysis in the USA has it all:

  • Exhaustive Arc flash reports
  • Compliance with standards
  • Arc Flash PPE recommendations
  • Arc Flash Labeling
  • Arc Flash Boundary
